title: Translate a delivered tutorial (domain + language reviewer)
context: Translating a delivered tutorial into another language widens its reuse and benefits researchers who are more comfortable in a language other than English — a significant consideration in the global cancer research community. riskTier is medium because translating biological/clinical claims and the 'not medical advice' disclaimer requires both a domain reviewer (to confirm the scientific accuracy of the translation) and a language reviewer (a fluent native speaker in the target language). The source tutorial must already be delivered, reproducible in CI, and carry all mandatory elements.
objective: Translate one delivered tutorial into a target language, with a language reviewer (native-fluent) and a domain reviewer (verifies biological/clinical accuracy of the translated text) signing off, and the 'not medical advice' disclaimer accurately rendered in the target language.
acceptanceCriteria: Source tutorial is a fully delivered, CI-green, domain-reviewed English tutorial from the project's prior contributions.
Translation is produced by a contributor fluent in the target language, or with native-language review.
A domain reviewer (bioinformatician or oncologist as appropriate) confirms biological/clinical claims are accurately conveyed in the translation.
The 'research/education use — not medical advice' disclaimer is translated and prominent in the target language.
All data-provenance attributions and citation references are preserved accurately in the translation.
Language reviewer (native or near-native speaker of the target language) sign-off obtained.
Translation is committed and accessible for distribution, with the source tutorial referenced.
output: A delivered tutorial translated into at least one additional language, with domain and language reviewer sign-offs, accurate 'not medical advice' disclaimer, and all provenance/citations preserved — widening the tutorial's reuse to non-English-speaking cancer researchers.
